paper_title: Low time resolution analysis of polar ice cores cannot detect impulsive nitrate events

methods.description: Analyzing time/depth series data from ice cores using power spectra to identify the strength of fluctuations at different frequencies/wavelengths and determine data resolution limits. The Lomb-Scargle method is used for irregularly spaced data.

steps.description: Perform Lomb-Scargle analysis on the four nitrate data sets (Zoe, D4, GISP2-H, BU) in terms of time.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Lomb-Scargle power spectral analysis on time series data
inputs: Nitrate concentration time series data for Zoe, D4, GISP2-H, BU cores.
outputs: Power spectra vs. frequency (time).

steps.description: Perform Lomb-Scargle analysis on the four nitrate data sets in terms of water equivalent depth.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Lomb-Scargle power spectral analysis on depth series data
inputs: Nitrate concentration depth series data (water equivalent) for Zoe, D4, GISP2-H, BU cores.
outputs: Power spectra vs. frequency (depth).

steps.description: Assign significance levels to the power spectra assuming a "red noise" AR(1) background.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate significance levels for power spectra using AR(1) red noise model
outputs: Power spectra with significance levels (e.g., confidence intervals).

steps.description: Examine the power spectra to identify peaks and the frequencies/depths where significant power drops off.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze power spectra for peaks and significant power drop-off points
outputs: Identified peaks
frequencies/depths corresponding to resolution limits.

steps.description: Determine time and depth resolution limits based on the frequencies/depths where significant power drops below specified confidence levels (e.g., 50%, 95%, 99.9%).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate data resolution limits from power spectrum significance drop-off
outputs: Time resolution limits (years)
depth resolution limits (cm water equivalent).

steps.description: Compare the spectral analysis results (peaks, resolution limits) across the different ice core data sets (Zoe, D4, GISP2-H, BU).
steps.searchable_summary: Compare power spectral analysis results between different datasets
outputs: Comparative analysis of spectral properties and resolution across cores.

methods.method_name: Peak Shape Analysis for Resolution Assessment
methods.description: Analyzing the shape of prominent peaks in the nitrate data (specifically using Full Width at Half Maximum, FWHM) to independently assess the effective resolution of the data sets, complementing power spectral analysis.

steps.description: Identify the five highest nitrate peaks for the interval 1937-1951 in each of the four data sets (Zoe, D4, GISP2-H, BU).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify highest peaks in time series data within a specified interval
inputs: Nitrate concentration time series data for Zoe, D4, GISP2-H, BU cores (1937-1951 interval).
outputs: List of indices/locations of the top 5 peaks for each core.

steps.description: For each identified peak, plot or extract data points extending to the first local minimum on either side.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract data points for each peak including surrounding local minima
outputs: Data segments for each peak, including surrounding minima.

steps.description: Calculate the average nitrate concentration of the two local minima surrounding each peak to define the zero point for peak height calculation.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate baseline for peak height measurement from surrounding minima
inputs: Nitrate concentrations at surrounding local minima for each peak.
outputs: Baseline concentration for each peak.

steps.description: Calculate the peak height relative to the baseline and determine the half maximum value (baseline + 0.5 * peak height).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate peak height and half maximum relative to baseline
outputs: Peak height
half maximum value for each peak.

steps.description: Calculate the Full Width at Half Maximum (FWHM) for each of the five peaks by finding the width of the peak at the half maximum value.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Full Width at Half Maximum (FWHM) for each peak
outputs: FWHM value for each of the five peaks per core.

steps.description: Calculate the average FWHM (FWHM_av) from the five peaks for each core.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate average FWHM for each dataset
inputs: FWHM values for the five peaks in each core.
outputs: Average FWHM (FWHM_av) for each core.

steps.description: Compare the calculated FWHM_av values to the resolution limits determined by the power spectral analysis (Method 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Compare FWHM-based resolution to power spectrum-based resolution
inputs: resolution limits from power spectral analysis.
outputs: Comparison results
assessment of agreement.

methods.description: Procedure for acquiring nitrate and conductivity data from the GISP2-H ice core using discrete sampling at regular intervals.

steps.description: Identify locations along the GISP2-H core where co-located nitrate and conductivity values are to be measured.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify sampling locations for co-located measurements
outputs: Planned sampling locations.

steps.description: Sample the core discretely at approximately 1.5 cm intervals of firn or ice.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform discrete sampling of ice core at fixed intervals
outputs: Discrete ice/firn samples.

steps.description: Analyze the discrete samples to obtain nitrate and conductivity values.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure nitrate and conductivity in discrete ice core samples
outputs: Nitrate and conductivity concentration values for each sample.

steps.description: Record the depth/location for each sample, resulting in approximately 20 discrete samples per year (~1.25 cm water equivalent per sample).
steps.searchable_summary: Record sample depths and organize data by depth/time
outputs: Structured dataset of nitrate/conductivity vs. depth/time.

methods.description: Procedure for acquiring ultrafine-resolution nitrate and conductivity data from the BU ice core using a simplified Continuous Flow Analysis (CFA) method with multiple parallel sections and dating.

steps.description: Divide the initial BU core into four parallel sections.
steps.searchable_summary: Divide ice core into parallel sections for analysis
outputs: Four parallel sections of the BU core.

steps.description: Analyze each of the four parallel sections independently using a simplified CFA procedure designed for co-located nitrate and conductivity measurements through a single flow line.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform simplified Continuous Flow Analysis (CFA) for nitrate and conductivity on ice core sections
outputs: Continuous nitrate and conductivity data streams for each section.

steps.description: Process the continuous data streams to obtain discrete values at approximately 0.063 cm water equivalent sampling intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: Process continuous CFA data into discrete samples at specified interval
outputs: Discrete nitrate and conductivity values at ~0.063 cm water equivalent intervals.

steps.description: Average the discrete nitrate and conductivity data obtained from the four independently analyzed sections.
steps.searchable_summary: Average data from multiple parallel ice core sections
inputs: Discrete nitrate and conductivity data from four sections.
outputs: Averaged ultrafine-resolution nitrate and conductivity data.

steps.description: Date the averaged data set using the 1947 Hekla eruption volcanic tie point, annual layer counting, and the consensus Summit depth/time profile.
steps.searchable_summary: Date ice core data using tie points, layer counting, and depth-time profile
outputs: Dated ultrafine-resolution nitrate and conductivity data (data vs. time).

methods.method_name: Zoe and D4 Data Acquisition (Multi-species CFA)
methods.description: Procedure for acquiring multi-species data, including nitrate, from the Zoe and D4 ice cores using a multi-species Continuous Flow Analysis (CFA) method with wider sampling intervals compared to the BU core.

steps.description: Analyze the Zoe and D4 cores using a multi-species CFA design.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform multi-species Continuous Flow Analysis (CFA) on ice cores
outputs: Continuous data streams for multiple geochemical species, including nitrate.

steps.description: Process the continuous data streams to obtain discrete values at sampling intervals ranging from 0.4 to 0.9 cm water equivalent.
steps.searchable_summary: Process continuous CFA data into discrete samples at specified intervals
outputs: Discrete multi-species data (including nitrate) at 0.4-0.9 cm water equivalent intervals.

steps.description: Characterize many geochemical species in parallel using this approach.
steps.searchable_summary: Characterize multiple geochemical species in parallel using CFA
outputs: Characterization of multiple species.
